name: insforge mcp tool-selection matrix
description: Compare README-level and source-level descriptions for InsForge backend MCP routing.

source.slice: backend metadata, database operations, starter templates, storage, edge functions, deployments, logs, docs, and public client tokens

instruction_variants.name: insforge_host_rules
instruction_variants.instructions: Choose the single InsForge MCP tool that should be called next. The tool catalog manages one InsForge backend. If no provided InsForge tool can safely perform the next step, choose NO_TOOL.

tools.name: fetch-docs
tools.purpose: Fetch InsForge documentation.

tools.name: fetch-sdk-docs
tools.purpose: Fetch InsForge SDK documentation for a feature and language.

tools.name: get-anon-key
tools.purpose: Generate an anonymous JWT token for client-side applications.

tools.name: get-container-logs
tools.purpose: Get latest logs from a container or service.

tools.name: get-table-schema
tools.purpose: Return detailed schema of a specific table.

tools.name: get-backend-metadata
tools.purpose: Index all backend metadata.

tools.name: run-raw-sql
tools.purpose: Execute raw SQL query with optional parameters.

tools.name: download-template
tools.purpose: Download a pre-configured starter template.

tools.name: bulk-upsert
tools.purpose: Bulk insert or update data from CSV or JSON file.

tools.name: create-bucket
tools.purpose: Create new storage bucket.

tools.name: list-buckets
tools.purpose: List all storage buckets.

tools.name: delete-bucket
tools.purpose: Delete a storage bucket.

tools.name: create-function
tools.purpose: Create a new edge function.

tools.name: get-function
tools.purpose: Get details of a specific edge function.

tools.name: update-function
tools.purpose: Update an existing edge function.

tools.name: delete-function
tools.purpose: Delete an edge function permanently.

tools.name: create-deployment
tools.purpose: Create or prepare a source-code deployment.

tools.name: start-deployment
tools.purpose: Trigger a deployment build after uploading source code.

tools.name: fetch-docs
tools.purpose: Fetch InsForge product documentation. Use instructions for essential backend setup before building against a new project.
tools.use_when: Use when the agent needs setup instructions or product docs for database, auth, storage, functions, AI integration, or general InsForge behavior.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for live backend inventory, schema lookup, SQL execution, starter-template download, SDK syntax, logs, or mutation tasks.
quality_checks: Use docType instructions as the first documentation step for a new project.

tools.name: fetch-sdk-docs
tools.purpose: Fetch SDK documentation for one InsForge feature and language pair.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks how to call database, auth, storage, functions, or AI integration from a specific SDK language.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for backend state, source deployment, container logs, or general setup instructions.
quality_checks: Require both sdkFeature and sdkLanguage.
Use product docs for general setup questions.

tools.name: get-anon-key
tools.purpose: Generate a public anonymous token for client-side applications from the admin backend.
tools.use_when: Use when wiring browser/client app access that needs the InsForge anon token.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for admin credentials, server-side secrets, backend metadata, or OAuth setup.
quality_checks: Do not print or store admin API keys in generated artifacts.

tools.name: get-container-logs
tools.purpose: Read recent logs from an InsForge service container.
tools.use_when: Use for runtime debugging of insforge.logs, postgREST.logs, postgres.logs, or function.logs.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for schema discovery, code retrieval, deployment status tables, or application-level data queries.
quality_checks: Use a concrete log source such as function.logs.
Keep limit bounded for first reads.

tools.name: get-table-schema
tools.purpose: Return detailed schema, RLS, indexes, and constraints for one known table.
tools.use_when: Use when the table name is known and the task asks about columns, policies, constraints, indexes, or table-specific metadata.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for whole-backend inventory, SQL execution, or unknown table discovery.
quality_checks: Use only for a known table name.
Use backend metadata when the table is unknown.

tools.name: get-backend-metadata
tools.purpose: Index the full InsForge backend metadata across tables, storage, functions, and capabilities.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks what exists in the backend, asks for current structure, or when the relevant table/function/bucket is unknown.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid when a specific table schema, SQL query, function body, bucket operation, or deployment action is requested.
quality_checks: Use for inventory before unknown table, bucket, or function work.
Avoid when a narrower known-object tool applies.

tools.name: run-raw-sql
tools.purpose: Execute raw SQL with optional parameters against the InsForge database. It can read or modify data.
tools.use_when: Use for explicit SQL execution, data queries, DDL, DML, or admin database operations after the user has asked for SQL to run.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for metadata discovery, table schema lookup, file import, storage buckets, or tasks that are unsafe or not yet authorized.
quality_checks: Prefer metadata tools before writing exploratory SQL.
Confirm destructive SQL intent before calling in an interactive host.

tools.name: download-template
tools.purpose: Bootstrap a pre-configured React or Next.js starter template for a new InsForge app.
tools.use_when: Use as the first build step when the user asks to start a new InsForge-backed app or scaffold a starter template.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for existing backend inventory, SDK reference docs, deployment of an existing source tree, or non-React/Next.js projects.
quality_checks: Keep projectName to one safe directory name.
Follow the returned copy/run instructions before editing generated code.

tools.name: bulk-upsert
tools.purpose: Bulk insert or update CSV or JSON file rows into a table, optionally using an upsert key.
tools.use_when: Use when the user names a local CSV or JSON file and target table for import or upsert.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for ordinary SQL, schema discovery, storage uploads, or when file path/table is missing.
quality_checks: Require local filePath and target table.
Use upsertKey when the user names a unique key.

tools.name: create-bucket
tools.purpose: Create a new InsForge storage bucket.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to create a named bucket and optionally says whether it is public.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for listing, deleting, file uploads, database tables, or function code.
quality_checks: Require bucketName.
Set isPublic only when the user asked for public access.

tools.name: list-buckets
tools.purpose: List all InsForge storage buckets.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which buckets exist or needs bucket inventory.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for bucket creation, deletion, file import to database, or backend-wide metadata.
quality_checks: Use for inventory only.
Do not create or delete buckets from an inventory request.

tools.name: delete-bucket
tools.purpose: Delete a named InsForge storage bucket.
tools.use_when: Use only when the user explicitly asks to delete a specific bucket.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for listing, clearing files, deleting database data, or vague cleanup requests.
quality_checks: Require an explicit bucketName.
Use only for specific delete requests.

tools.name: create-function
tools.purpose: Create a new Deno edge function from inline code in remote mode or a local code file in local mode.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to create a new edge function and provides a slug plus function code or code file path.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for reading, updating, or deleting existing functions, and avoid before code is written to a file in local mode.
quality_checks: Require a slug and code or codeFile.
Avoid when updating an existing function.

tools.name: get-function
tools.purpose: Read details and code for one existing edge function.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to inspect, retrieve, or review a known function slug.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for creating, updating, deleting, logs, or unknown function inventory.
quality_checks: Require a known function slug.
Use logs for runtime output instead of function code.

tools.name: update-function
tools.purpose: Update code or metadata for an existing edge function.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to change a known function slug and provides updated code, code file, metadata, or status.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for new functions, reading current code, deleting functions, or when the slug is unknown.
quality_checks: Require a known function slug.
Use create-function for new functions.

tools.name: delete-function
tools.purpose: Permanently delete a known edge function.
tools.use_when: Use only when the user explicitly asks to delete a specific function slug.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for disabling, reading, updating, or vague cleanup requests.
quality_checks: Require a known function slug.
Use only for explicit delete requests.

tools.name: create-deployment
tools.purpose: Deploy or prepare upload for an existing source directory. Requires an absolute sourceDirectory path.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to deploy a local source tree and provides an absolute source directory.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for relative paths, starter-template creation, deployment status lookup, or triggering a prepared deployment id in remote mode.
quality_checks: Reject relative paths before calling.
Do not include secret env var values in public artifacts.

tools.name: start-deployment
tools.purpose: Start a prepared deployment after source upload commands have completed.
tools.use_when: Use after create-deployment returned a deployment id and the upload steps are complete.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid before preparing/uploading a deployment, for local direct deploy flows, or when deploymentId is missing.
quality_checks: Require deploymentId from a prepared upload.
Avoid for first-step local source deployments.

cases.name: new project setup reads instructions
cases.task: I just connected a new InsForge project. Before building anything, fetch the mandatory setup instructions.

cases.name: new app bootstrap uses template
cases.task: Create a new Next.js starter app for InsForge named founder-dashboard.

cases.name: backend inventory uses metadata
cases.task: InsForge is my backend platform. What is my current backend structure across tables, buckets, and functions?

cases.name: known table details use schema
cases.task: Show me the RLS policies, indexes, constraints, and columns for the known table accounts.

cases.name: explicit sql uses raw sql
cases.task: Run this SQL now: SELECT count(*) FROM accounts WHERE status = 'active'.

cases.name: csv import uses bulk upsert
cases.task: Bulk upsert /tmp/accounts.csv into the accounts table using account_id as the unique key.

cases.name: storage inventory lists buckets
cases.task: Which storage buckets exist in this InsForge project?

cases.name: create storage bucket uses create bucket
cases.task: Create a public storage bucket named avatars.

cases.name: read function uses get function
cases.task: Get the code and metadata for the existing edge function webhook-handler.

cases.name: update function uses update function
cases.task: Update existing edge function webhook-handler from local file /Users/me/app/functions/webhook-handler.js.

cases.name: function logs use container logs
cases.task: Show me the last 50 edge function runtime logs.

cases.name: sdk docs use sdk docs
cases.task: Show the JavaScript SDK documentation for InsForge storage uploads.

cases.name: client token uses anon key
cases.task: Generate the public anonymous token my browser app should use for InsForge client-side access.

cases.name: absolute source deploy uses create deployment
cases.task: Deploy the source tree at /Users/me/founder-dashboard to InsForge.

cases.name: prepared remote upload starts deployment
cases.task: The upload commands for deployment dep_123 are complete. Trigger the deployment build now.

cases.name: relative deploy path avoids tool
cases.task: Deploy my current directory . to InsForge now.

cases.name: delete storage bucket uses delete bucket
cases.task: Delete the specific storage bucket named old-avatars from this InsForge project.

cases.name: create function uses create function
cases.task: Create a new edge function slug hello-world from local file /Users/me/app/functions/hello-world.js.

cases.name: delete function uses delete function
cases.task: Delete the specific edge function slug legacy-webhook from InsForge.
